meaning: n.[矿]海泡石,海泡石的烟斗
examples: The conditions and mechanism of removing fluoride ions from water by activated meerschaum have been investigated in this paper.
The modified meerschaum was first used to recover gallium from acid lixivium of zinc residues and the experimental results were satisfactory.
Meerschaum starts out white, then blushes pink, turns tan, and deepens into shades of brown.
Pipe collector Charles Strom loves his little mermaid cheroot holder, created by legendary meerschaum carver Gustave Fischer in the early 20th century.
Meerschaum, a claylike material mined in Turkey, darkens gradually when smoked.
